message:
fix(install): add ~/.local/bin to PATH early in installer

The native Claude Code installer puts the binary at ~/.local/bin/claude.
Adding ~/.local/bin to PATH at the top of install.sh ensures:
- The binary is immediately discoverable after installation
- Subsequent verification steps can find claude
- No PATH conflicts with bun-installed versions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -66,6 +66,10 @@ if [[ -n "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" && -f "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" ]]; then
     SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
 fi
 
+# Early PATH setup: ensure ~/.local/bin is available for native installers (e.g., Claude Code)
+# This is critical because the Claude native installer puts the binary at ~/.local/bin/claude
+export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
+
 # Default options
 YES_MODE=false
 DRY_RUN=false
